probes.name: basic_commit_suggestion_run
probes.prompt: Hey, can you run the suggestion engine on the techtree repo? Look at the recent commits and active PRs and give me feature suggestions. I want at least 4 ideas with implementation details, saved as a proper analysis run.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a feature-suggestion engine for a codebase. A GOOD response reads recent commit history and open PRs from the techtree repo, avoids suggesting features already being worked on in active PRs, produces 4+ actionable feature suggestions each with concrete implementation specs (files to modify, approach, dependencies), and saves the result as a structured analysis run.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs #10, #19 (checking tasks/status) and #20 (running a specific agent pipeline), adapted to the suggestion engine role.

probes.name: polish_commit_trend_analysis
probes.prompt: Możesz sprawdzić ostatnie commity w techtree i zasugerować nowe ficzery? Chodzi mi o to żebyś przeanalizował trendy, zobaczył co się ostatnio zmieniało i zaproponował 5 pomysłów z pełną specyfiką implementacji. Zwróć uwagę żeby nie dublować rzeczy z aktywnych PR-ów.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a feature-suggestion engine for a codebase. A GOOD response analyzes recent commit trends in the techtree repository, identifies patterns in what has been changing, explicitly checks active PRs to avoid duplicating work, outputs exactly 5 feature suggestions in Polish or English each with full implementation specifications (affected modules, code approach, edge cases), and persists the analysis as a saved run.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs #5, #23 (checking something specific with constraints) and #27 (Polish language task), adapted for commit analysis.

probes.name: focused_area_suggestions
probes.prompt: Can you analyze the techtree repo commits from the last two weeks focusing on the visualization and UI layer? I noticed a lot of changes there recently. Give me 3-7 suggestions but specifically for improving the visualization components — charts, dashboards, that kind of thing. Include what files would need to change and a rough implementation plan for each. Make sure you check open PRs first so we don't duplicate work.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a feature-suggestion engine for a codebase. A GOOD response scopes its analysis to visualization/UI-related commits, filters commit data to the relevant subsystem, checks active PRs for overlapping UI/visualization work, produces 3-7 targeted suggestions each naming specific files, components, and implementation steps for visualization improvements, and saves the focused analysis run.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs #7, #18 (finding specific examples/focused search) and #31 (finding interesting details about a specific angle).

probes.name: polish_avoid_duplicates_with_context
probes.prompt: Hej, potrzebuję nowych propozycji ficzerów do techtree. Ale najpierw sprawdź wszystkie otwarte PR-y bo nie chcę żebyś proponował coś co już ktoś robi. Potem przejrzyj commity z tego tygodnia i na tej podstawie wymyśl około 6 propozycji. Do każdej dodaj opis implementacji: jakie pliki, jakie zmiany, jakie zależności. Zapisz to jako analysis run.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a feature-suggestion engine for a codebase. A GOOD response first enumerates active PRs and their scopes, explicitly excludes those topics, then reviews the week's commits to identify gaps and opportunities, generates approximately 6 non-duplicate feature suggestions each with a complete implementation spec (target files, change description, dependency considerations), and persists the output as a named analysis run.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs #1 (checking off completed work), #24 (rescheduling/checking conversations), and #37 (coherent reporting), combined with the agent's core deduplication responsibility.

probes.name: suggestions_with_priority_and_effort
probes.prompt: Run a full suggestion analysis on the techtree codebase — commits, PRs, the whole thing. I want 5 feature ideas but this time also rank them by how useful they'd be and how much effort they'd take. For each suggestion give me: the feature name, why it matters based on recent commit patterns, the full implementation spec with files and approach, and a rough effort estimate (small/medium/large). Save it as an analysis run I can come back to.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a feature-suggestion engine for a codebase. A GOOD response performs a comprehensive analysis of commits and PRs, produces exactly 5 ranked feature suggestions, each containing: a descriptive name, a justification grounded in observed commit patterns/trends, a full implementation specification (files, approach, dependencies), and a realistic effort classification, all saved as a retrievable analysis run.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by msgs #6 (iteration on success), #14 (reminding about a task), and #22 (checking in on progress), reflecting the user's pattern of wanting structured, actionable output with persistence.
